author: aarshshahoffchain
content: <@1057495364564946944>  Why is getBlobsV3 on sparse blob pool slower than getBlobsV4 ?

author: bosulmun
content: Because the sparse blobpool does not keep blobs in the mempool(only cells), while getBlobsV3 expects them. So we need to recover them on demand. Regarding this problem I want to take a closer look and see whether we can cache some of them

author: marcopolo__
content:
Here's a shadow (ethshadow) simulation of a 128 node network with 64 supernodes and split between prysm and lighthouse with different getBlobs latencies.

[250ms latency](http://149.248.12.203:3000/d/bfbnwkabmnoxsb/marco-cell-level-deltas-devnet-dashboard?orgId=1&from=2000-01-01T00:00:00.000Z&to=2000-01-01T00:40:00.000Z&timezone=browser)

[0ms latency](http://149.248.12.203:3000/d/bfbnwkabmnoxsb/marco-cell-level-deltas-devnet-dashboard?orgId=1&from=2000-01-01T00:00:00.000Z&to=2000-01-01T00:40:00.000Z&timezone=browser)

Don't read too much into this yet, I need to double check the setup. But I'm very happy that I was able to get a simulation with our actual code running.

author: marcopolo__
content: I think we can scale this up to 1000 simulated nodes. and even more if we rent a beefy server. Did you know you can rent ec2 instances that have 32TB of ram?
